danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite appearance in world space; hostile entity clustering
danger_signals.meaning: Areas plagued by creatures represent corrupted or dangerous zones requiring cleansing

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation of environment, hostile creature presence
danger_signals.meaning: World state indicates active threats to crown authority and civilized lands

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy movement toward party, combat animations, collision states
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring player intervention; no turn-based safety buffer

safety_signals.visual_form: Structured buildings, NPC clusters, organized architecture
safety_signals.meaning: Civilized spaces where crown authority is maintained

safety_signals.visual_form: Companion character positioning and behavior adjustments
safety_signals.meaning: Player is not alone; delegated threat management provides safety margin

safety_signals.visual_form: Inventory gump windows, dialogue trees, menu overlays
safety_signals.meaning: Player can pause world threats to manage resources and decisions

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Towns, settlements, organized structures representing kingdom control

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Monster sprites, enemy creature types with distinct visual appearances
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Infested areas, corrupted zones, dangerous wilderness regions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental storytelling through interactive object placement; deceptions visible in NPC positioning and dialogue
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC behavior reflects plot progression; dialogue tree options change based on learned plot keywords

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: High density of hostile entity sprites in specific regions; corrupted environmental appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy entities cluster and patrol; hostile AI aggression toward party

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Degraded or corrupted town/settlement visuals; reduced NPC organization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs exhibit distress; reduced structured movement patterns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental restoration; normalized NPC positioning and town structure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs return to organized routines; hostile entities absent from cleared zones

environmental_storytelling.example: Items can be dragged, dropped, stacked, and rearranged to reveal story context (e.g., item arrangement shows narrative); cooking bread, forging weapons, painting portraiture—all interactive object-based storytelling

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Stacked objects and multi-layered world create puzzle solutions and narrative barriers; inaccessible areas until player constructs item stairs

environmental_storytelling.example: Character location and state communicate plot status; keyword-gated dialogue prevents premature narrative reveals, controlling information discovery
Gameworld occupies entire screen (DOS original), removing text/UI clutter—world environment is primary communication channel for state and danger

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: AI autonomously selects threat targets; stronger characters waylay toughest opponents; vulnerable characters maintain distance
AI self-selects weapon swaps from inventory; may drop weapons during retreat to signal disengagement
Real-time movement toward party; collision-based combat engagement; sprite animations indicate attack state
Dialogue tree responses change based on keyword discovery; positioning reflects plot progression; distressed behavior during threat states
Double-click opens inventory gump; game pauses—visual signal that player has control window over world threats

notes: Ultima VII SNES represents the paradigm shift from grid-based to continuous-space world communication. World state is conveyed through: (1) environmental density and corruption visuals, (2) interactive object arrangements as narrative language, (3) real-time NPC/creature behavior reflecting political/threat situations, (4) dialogue tree gating revealing plot layers progressively, and (5) full-screen world dominance eliminating UI barriers—the gameworld itself becomes the primary communication medium. Faction identity is implicit rather than explicit (no color/symbol system noted); distinction is behavioral—organized civilized NPCs vs. hostile creature swarms. Danger is communicated through entity proximity, real-time combat state, and environmental degradation. Safety manifests through pause-capable systems and delegated AI party control. The game's interactivity (baking, forging, playing instruments, portrait painting) makes object states part of environmental storytelling.
